location.office_time_required: Monday, Tuesday, and Thursday in office

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in Product Management
Referred to explicitly as 'Staff Product Manager' in the JD body
Expected to mentor others and shape team morale
Accountable for roadmap and business impact on a new $100M+ line of business

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 7+ years of experience in Product Management.
Systems thinker: You feel just as comfortable modeling data relationships in a technical diagram, just as well as you can partner with Design to walk stakeholders through a holistic UX flow.
Strategic: You inspire the company by creating bold, intuitive work connections within the platform and scoping them to solve the most critical customer problems.
Get Stuff Done: You create, flex, and evangelize a roadmap; thoughtfully break down projects to MVPs + subsequent iterations and ship with high velocity for business impact.
Customer-centric: You analyze our top customer personas; dig into the roots of customer needs; synthesize research to gain a deep understanding and narrow definition of a problem.

Communication and collaboration: You partner with cross-functional teammates to deliver high-quality results. You speak with clarity and write sharp product specs. You are highly comfortable getting into deep technical discussions with engineers about the pros and cons of different approaches, and you pair with design on complex user flows.
Grow Team Asana: You are a mentor for others; create great team morale among other functions and PMs; and share PM best practices you've cultivated to date.
Growth Mindset: You lead with curiosity and are open to feedback in order to learn.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Background in building in environments with complex data models, human-centric UX, and B2B SaaS experience preferred.

scope.surface: Agentic software development lifecycle product — Asana's new product and bet on a $100M+ line of business
primary_metrics_inferred: Business impact / revenue growth toward $100M+ line of business
Customer satisfaction across hypergrowth startups to global enterprises
core_responsibilities: Deliver a high-craft product to a new kind of customer for Asana
Build product with strong passion and operational maturity
Create a product strategy to balance multiple priorities and use cases across customers, product teams, and enterprise-level compliance
Negotiate with partner teams and deliver a compelling roadmap
Maximize clarity by bringing a data-driven mindset to setting concrete goals and milestones
Define how modern teams plan, sequence, and ship work in an AI-native world
Validate bets directly with demanding customers—from hypergrowth startups to global enterprises

compensation.equity_form: equity mentioned as possible component of compensation package

green_flags: Compensation range disclosed in PLN (37,000–46,000 PLN gross/month)
Explicitly a greenfield, category-defining product — not optimizing an inherited roadmap
Clear product surface: agentic software development lifecycle, framed as $100M+ bet
Role explicitly titled 'Staff Product Manager' in the body — seniority is clear
Encourages applications even if not every requirement is met
Rich benefits package including health insurance, gym, fertility support, mental health support
50% tax deductible costs option (author's rights) for applicable roles in Poland
red_flags: Role is Warsaw-based only with mandatory in-office days (Mon, Tue, Thu) — eliminates remote candidates
Compensation stated in PLN only; USD equivalent not provided (requires currency conversion)
Work authorization and visa sponsorship not addressed in the JD
JD title says 'Product Manager' but body refers to the role as 'Staff Product Manager' — mild inconsistency

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 'Staff Product Manager' label in body + 7+ years requirement + mentorship expectations)

ambiguities: Role title in metadata is 'Product Manager' but the JD body refers to the role as 'Staff Product Manager'. Treated as Staff PM based on body text.
7+ years requirement appears in a section labeled 'About you' rather than 'Required' — classified as hard per JD language conventions since it is unconditional.
B2B SaaS and complex data models experience is marked 'preferred' but appears in the same bullet as the 7+ year requirement — classified as soft/nice-to-have.
Visa sponsorship eligibility not stated; unclear if non-EU candidates would be considered for the Warsaw office.
